prompt: Design a synchronous 8-bit by 8-bit sequential shift-add multiplier in Verilog-2001. Interface: a clock (clk), a synchronous active-high reset (reset), a start input (start), two 8-bit operand inputs (a and b), a 16-bit product output (product), and a done output. The multiplier uses the iterative shift-and-add algorithm over 8 clock cycles, sequenced by a small control FSM with three states: IDLE, BUSY, and DONE. When start is asserted in IDLE it loads the operands and begins; on each BUSY cycle it adds the shifted multiplicand into the product accumulator when the current low bit of the multiplier is 1, shifts the multiplicand left by one and the multiplier right by one, and decrements an iteration counter; after 8 cycles it enters DONE and presents the full 16-bit product. done is a COMBINATIONAL flag that is high exactly in the DONE state. On reset the FSM returns to IDLE with the product and datapath registers cleared. Express the left/right shifts as multiply/divide by 2 and the low-bit test as modulo 2, since the design uses no shift or bit-select operators.
